summary:
Overall UK risk remains Elevated. Economic conditions are still sensitive to energy and traded-goods costs: ONS reports CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026, with motor fuels the largest upward contributor to the monthly change in the CPI annual rate. Brent crude is around the mid-$100s per barrel, keeping upside risk to transport and input costs.

Security and resilience pressures remain elevated. The Russia–Ukraine war continues to generate escalation and supply-route risk for Europe (including recent reported drone strikes in Odesa). Domestically, cyber risk remains elevated with the NCSC urging immediate action on Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities (CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368). UK natural-hazard risk is comparatively contained, with no active Met Office weather warnings and Environment Agency headline flood alerts remaining low.

categories.name: Economic / Financial
categories.rationale: Economic risk remains Elevated: inflation is still above target and sensitive to fuel/energy costs. ONS reports CPI inflation at 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026 and notes motor fuels as the largest upward contributor to the monthly change in the CPI annual rate; Brent remains around the mid-$100s per barrel, maintaining upside risk to transport and input costs.
drivers: ONS: CPI inflation 3.3% (12 months to March 2026); motor fuels largest upward contribution to monthly change in CPI annual rate. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026
Trading Economics: Brent crude around 106.55 USD/bbl (Apr 27, 2026). https://tradingeconomics.com/commodity/brent-crude-oil

Ofgem: energy price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained
OBR (March 2026 EFO): real GDP growth forecast 1.1% in 2026. https://obr.uk/efo/economic-and-fiscal-outlook-march-2026/

categories.name: Military / Security
categories.rationale: European security remains Elevated due to ongoing Russia–Ukraine conflict activity and associated escalation/spillover risk; UK domestic terrorism threat level remains SUBSTANTIAL.
drivers: Reuters: Russian drone attack on Odesa killed an elderly couple and injured others (reported Apr 24, 2026). https://www.reuters.com/world/europe/russian-attack-ukraines-odesa-kills-married-couple-officials-say-2026-04-24/

GOV.UK: Defence Secretary described a recent operation responding to increased Russian submarine activity north of the UK and around undersea infrastructure (speech published Apr 9, 2026). https://www.gov.uk/government/speeches/defence-secretary-no9-speech-09-april-2026
MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (an attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

categories.name: Political / Governance
categories.rationale: No acute UK governance instability signal identified in reviewed official sources, but political fragmentation remains a watch factor.
drivers: GOV.UK (31 Mar 2026): press release references the PM chairing a COBR(M) meeting (no new emergency measures identified in reviewed sources today). https://www.gov.uk/government/news/new-measures-coming-in-to-ease-cost-of-living-pressure-1-april-2026

YouGov: Westminster voting intention (fieldwork 19–20 Apr 2026) shows Reform UK leading at 27% with Greens 17%, Conservatives 17%, Labour 16%, Lib Dems 14%. https://yougov.com/en-gb/articles/54580-voting-intention-19-20-april-2026-ref-27-grn-17-con-17-lab-16-ld-14

categories.name: Natural Hazards
categories.rationale: Near-term natural-hazard risk is Guarded: Met Office shows no active UK weather warnings, and Environment Agency flood risk remains limited with a low headline number of flood alerts.
drivers: Met Office: UK weather warnings page shows no active warnings (viewed 27 Apr 2026). https://weather.metoffice.gov.uk/warnings-and-advice/uk-warnings
Environment Agency (GOV.UK): flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 27 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

categories.name: Social Stability
categories.rationale: Social disruption risk remains Elevated due to ongoing London Underground industrial action with additional scheduled strike dates in May and June.
drivers: BBC: London Underground RMT strike action includes further 24-hour walkouts scheduled in May and June (published Apr 23, 2026). https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/cj94x99yex3o

NHS England: BMA resident doctors industrial action ran 7–13 April 2026 with planning guidance focused on patient safety and escalation processes. https://www.england.nhs.uk/long-read/industrial-action-bma-resident-doctors-april-2026-2/

categories.name: Critical Infrastructure / Cyber
categories.rationale: Cyber/CNI risk remains Elevated due to urgent patching and configuration checks for Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities highlighted by the NCSC; exposure is time-sensitive for internet-facing edge appliances.
drivers: NCSC: urges immediate action to mitigate Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway vulnerabilities CVE-2026-3055 and CVE-2026-4368. https://www.ncsc.gov.uk/news/vulnerabilities-affecting-citrix-netscaler-adc-gateway

indicators.name: UK CPI inflation (12 months to Mar 2026)
indicators.note: ONS: CPI inflation was 3.3% in the 12 months to March 2026. https://www.ons.gov.uk/economy/inflationandpriceindices/bulletins/consumerpriceinflation/march2026

indicators.name: Ofgem energy price cap (typical dual-fuel, Direct Debit)
indicators.note: Ofgem: price cap for 1 Apr–30 Jun 2026 is £1,641/year (typical dual-fuel household paying by Direct Debit). https://www.ofgem.gov.uk/information-consumers/energy-advice-households/energy-price-cap-explained

indicators.name: UK terrorism threat level
indicators.note: MI5: UK national terrorism threat level is SUBSTANTIAL (attack is likely). https://www.mi5.gov.uk/threats-and-advice/terrorism-threat-levels

indicators.name: England flood alerts (headline count)
indicators.note: Environment Agency flood alerts and warnings page shows 2 flood alerts (viewed 27 Apr 2026). https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings

changes_since_last_update: Timestamp advanced to 27 April 2026 05:00 UK; overall status held at Elevated.
Brent crude indicator refreshed from Reuters settlement pricing to Trading Economics snapshot (106.55 USD/bbl on Apr 27, 2026).
Natural Hazards view re-checked: Met Office still shows no active UK weather warnings; Environment Agency still shows 2 flood alerts.
Military/Security drivers expanded with GOV.UK Defence Secretary operational update on Russian submarine activity near UK waters (published Apr 9, 2026).
priority_watch_items: Fuel/inflation sensitivity: monitor whether sustained Brent strength feeds through into UK pump prices and near-term inflation expectations.
London transport disruption: monitor impacts as industrial action continues and as further strike dates in May/June approach.
Perimeter vulnerability exposure: confirm remediation progress for Citrix NetScaler ADC/Gateway and monitor for exploitation indicators.
European security: monitor for further Russia–Ukraine escalation affecting Black Sea-linked logistics and energy systems.
Flooding: monitor Environment Agency updates for any rapid escalation in local warnings/alerts following rainfall.
source_notes: This update prioritises UK official sources (ONS, Ofgem, MI5, NCSC, Met Office, Environment Agency, MOD/GOV.UK) with Reuters used for conflict reporting and the BBC for transport disruption reporting.
The Environment Agency flood-alert headline count was verified by page view due to variability in automated extraction. https://check-for-flooding.service.gov.uk/alerts-and-warnings
